role_delineation_check: The social worker is responsible for the clinical referral and securing the home health agency, whereas the Navigator verifies demographic accuracy in Atlantis, provides program education to the family, and flags any scheduling bottlenecks to the social worker without contacting external vendors or agencies.
narrative_summary: Martha Higgins, a 78-year-old patient recovering from a congestive heart failure exacerbation, was eager to return home but faced significant uncertainty when her home health agency referral remained unconfirmed for several days. This delay in agency acceptance created anxiety for Martha and her daughter, who were concerned about the lack of professional support upon arrival. The Patient Navigator supported the transition by educating the family on the program benefits, verifying that Martha's contact information was correctly updated for the incoming team, and maintaining close communication with the social worker to ensure the family remained informed while waiting for the agency's response.
demographic_audit_note: I verified Martha's primary mobile number and home address against the facility face sheet and discovered a digit was transposed in the phone number; I updated this in Atlantis to ensure the home health team and medical assistant can reach her successfully for the first visit.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home with home health services and medical assistant support.
v_card_flyer_status: I provided Martha and her daughter with the program flyer and assisted them in saving our digital contact card into Martha's smartphone to ensure they recognize our caller identification for all future coordination calls.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I spoke with Martha's daughter 24 hours prior to the expected discharge; she expressed some nervousness because the home health agency had only just accepted the case, but she felt more comfortable after I reviewed the first-day expectations for the medical assistant visit.
atlantis_final_sync: I documented the confirmed discharge date provided by the social worker and successfully logged the first medical assistant home visit in Atlantis for the day following Martha's return home.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met Martha at the bedside to introduce the Healing Partners program and documented her preference for a morning discharge in Atlantis for the social worker's awareness.

The social worker informed the Navigator that the home health referral was sent but the agency had not yet responded; the Navigator documented this stall in Atlantis and noted the family's concern regarding the lack of a set start-of-care date.
The Navigator sat with Martha's daughter to explain the specific role of the medical assistant during the first 24 hours at home and how they would bridge the gap until the home health nurse arrived.

Following a verbal update from the social worker that the agency finally accepted the case, the Navigator updated the Atlantis record with the assigned agency name and began scheduling the first home visit.

format_2_triples.situation: Martha's daughter is worried about missing calls from the home care team after they leave the facility.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator helped the daughter save the digital contact card into the patient's phone and explained that this would help her identify our team's calls immediately.

format_2_triples.situation: The home health agency has not responded to the facility referral for three consecutive days.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator alerted the social worker to the family's growing anxiety and documented the lack of an update in the Atlantis timeline to maintain a record of the delay.

format_2_triples.situation: The facility face sheet listed an old landline number for the patient that was no longer in service.
format_2_triples.action_taken: The Navigator obtained the correct mobile number directly from the family and updated the demographic record in Atlantis to ensure accurate communication.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator notes in Atlantis that the social worker has not yet provided an update on the agency referral and chooses to wait until the next morning to ask the social worker for a status update while continuing to monitor the patient's record.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the social worker's role as the owner of the referral process and avoids interfering with vendor relations, adhering to the wait default when information is pending.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator asks the social worker for a verbal status update on the referral and, upon learning it is still pending, documents the conversation in Atlantis and conducts a pulse call with the family to reassure them and review program benefits.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This action stays within the navigator's lane by gathering information from the appropriate internal source and focusing on family education and documentation without contacting external vendors.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator looks up the home health agency's phone number and calls their intake department directly to ask why the referral has not been accepted yet, then tells Martha that a nurse will definitely be at her house by noon on Monday.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the Navigator is prohibited from contacting external vendors or agencies and should never make clinical or scheduling promises that are outside their control or the social worker's domain.
